title: Making electrocompetent Agrobacterium tumefaciens
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ojgv","text":"This protocol yields about 5 ml of electrocompetent Agrobacterium tumefaciens cells, aliquotted into 80 ul you get 50-60 tubes out of it.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":52,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"11r7r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bpk1s","text":"Rifampicin is dark red and the colour will change over time, it does however NOT interfere with OD600 measurements, so you can use LB to blank. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8fgbc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: LB medium (for 1 litre combine 10 g Tryptone, 5 g Yeast Extrad and 10 g NaCl, adjust pH to 7.0 and autoclave) 10% glycerol solution (ice cold) H2O (ice cold) liquid nitrogen 50 - 60 x 1.5 ml tubes (pre-chill in fridge) 10 ml pipette tips 8 x 50 ml tubes (pre-chill in fridge) Your favourite strain of Agrobacterium tumefaciens grown on a plate Depending on your strain of Agrobacterium you'll need the following antibiotics: AGL1 : rifampicin 100 ng/ul LBA 4404 : rifampicin 100 ng/ul, streptomycin 100 ng/ul --> grows slow and clumps GV3101::pMP90 : rifampicin 100 ng/ul, gentamicin 25 ng/ul --> grows fastest of the three
Inoculate 10 ml LB (containing the appropriate antibiotics) with 1 colony of Agrobacterium tumefaciens in the morning and incubate at 28°C at 250 rpm for the rest of the day. 28 °C 3 mL, LB 05:00:00
In the evening inoculate 3 x 150 ml LB (containing the appropriate antibiotics) with 3 ml each of the culture grown throughout the day. 28 °C 450 mL, LB
Incubate at 28°C at 250 rpm until OD600 reaches 0.8 - 1.0 (This usually takes overnight, depending on Agro strain im might take longer). 28 °C 30:00:00
Check OD600, and combine all 3 cultures once OD600 reaches 0.8-1.0
Chill culture by putting it on ice for 15 minutes and swirling it regularly. From here on everything needs to happen on ice or at 4°C! 00:15:00 4 °C
Distribute culture into 8 x 50 ml tubes and pellet by centrifugation at 3000 - 4000 x g for 20-30 minutes at 4°C. 00:30:00 4 °C
Discard supernatant and resuspend each pellet in 10 ml ice cold H2O. 10 mL, H2O
Adjust volume to 50 ml each with ice cold H2O.
Pellet cells by centrifugation at 3000 - 4000 x g for 20-30 minutes at 4°C. 00:30:00 4 °C
Discard supernatant and resuspend each pellet in 10 ml ice cold H2O. 10 mL, H2O
Combine solutions into 4 x 50 ml tubes and adjust to 50 ml with ice cold H2O.
Pellet cells by centrifugation at 3000 - 4000 x g for 20-30 minutes at 4°C. 00:30:00 4 °C
Discard supernatant and resuspend each pellet in 10 ml ice cold H2O. 10 mL, H2O
Combine solutions into 2 x 50 ml tubes and adjust to 50 ml with ice cold H2O.
Pellet cells by centrifugation at 3000 - 4000 x g for 20-30 minutes at 4°C. 00:30:00 4 °C
Discard supernatant and resuspend each pellet in 10 ml ice cold H2O. 10 mL, H2O
Combine solutions into 1 x 50 ml tubes and adjust to 50 ml with ice cold H2O.
Pellet cells by centrifugation at 3000 - 4000 x g for 20-30 minutes at 4°C. (Use a counterbalence tube) 00:30:00 4 °C
Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in 4.5 ml ice-cold 10% glycerol. 4.5 mL, 10% glycerol
Dispense 80 ul aliquots into pre-chilled 1.5 ml tubes and flash freeze in liquid nitrogen.
Store aliquots at -80°C until use.
creator: Johannes Wolfram JWD Debler
